deep_dive.title: Sparsely gated tiny linear experts
deep_dive.abstract: Sparsity allows scaling model parameters without proportionally increasing computational cost. While mixture of experts (MoE) models are made increasingly sparse, individual experts typically remain large and dense. Here, we demonstrate that further increasing sparsity by shrinking each expert to consist of a single neuron and selecting a tiny fraction of many available neurons can improve compute efficiency and interpretability. Counterintuitively, the key to achieving both is removing the nonlinearity typically applied to the experts, resulting in a network of sparsely gated linear neurons (sgatlin). In an isoflop comparison, we find that replacing all transformer feedforward layers with sgatlin improves perplexity in language models across different compute budgets. At the same time, the sparsity and linearity of the resulting feedforward circuits present new opportunities for model interpretability. In a small-scale case study, we demonstrate that feedforward circuits in sgatlin can be interpreted without having to train additional replacement models. We find that they form semantically structured clusters and are causally implicated in factual recall. Our findings paint a possible path towards compute-efficient and interpretable transformer feedforward layers.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes sparsely gated linear experts for efficient MoE inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出稀疏门控线性专家以提升MoE推理效率
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出稀疏门控线性专家以提升MoE推理效率
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出稀疏门控线性专家以提升MoE推理效率
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes sparsely gated tiny linear experts (sgatlin) where each expert is a single neuron, achieving better perplexity under equal FLOPs compared to standard transformer FFNs, improving MoE inference efficiency.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有混合专家模型专家仍保持稠密结构，计算开销依然较高。本文提出稀疏门控线性专家（sgatlin），将每个专家压缩为单个线性神经元，并通过稀疏门控仅激活极少数专家，同时移除了非线性激活函数。在相等计算量下，sgatlin替代标准Transformer前馈层后大幅提升了语言模型困惑度。该工作表明，极端稀疏的线性专家结构能够在保持模型性能的同时显著提升推理效率，为MoE高效推理提供了新的设计方向。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有混合专家模型专家仍保持稠密结构，计算开销依然较高。本文提出稀疏门控线性专家（sgatlin），将每个专家压缩为单个线性神经元，并通过稀疏门控仅激活极少数专家，同时移除了非线性激活函数。在相等计算量下，sgatlin替代标准Transformer前馈层后大幅提升了语言模型困惑度。该工作表明，极端稀疏的线性专家结构能够在保持模型性能的同时显著提升推理效率，为MoE高效推理提供了新的设计方向。

quick_skim.title: Expert-Aware Causal Tracing of Factual Recall in Sparse MoE Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Causal tracing of factual recall has been studied predominantly in dense transformer language models, where interventions localize information flow to layers or feed-forward modules. Sparse mixture-of-experts (MoE) language models introduce a sharper question: when a factual prediction is mediated by a routed MoE block, which routed expert contributions matter? We formulate expert-aware causal tracing for sparse MoE language models. Using CounterFact facts, we first corrupt the model's factual preference by adding noise to subject-token embeddings, and then test whether clean MoE-block outputs or clean expert-level updates restore the true-vs-foil logit contrast. For Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base, a layer sweep selects and validates layer 44, and expert-level tracing identifies L44E069 as an expert repeatedly selected in the clean run whose held-out patch outperforms other active same-layer expert patches. For Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1, layer-level tracing validates a mid-layer signal, but the signal is not localized to the selected singleton expert; a coalition check instead recovers it with routed multi-expert updates. These results suggest that MoE factual tracing can be made expert-aware, while also showing that expert-level localization is model- and protocol-dependent rather than universal.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Expert-aware causal tracing for sparse MoE language models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 稀疏MoE语言模型的专家级因果追溯
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 稀疏MoE语言模型的专家级因果追溯
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 稀疏MoE语言模型的专家级因果追溯
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes expert-aware causal tracing to identify which routed expert contributions matter for factual recall in sparse MoE LMs, using counterfactual perturbations on Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base. Provide insights for MoE inference optimization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对稀疏MoE语言模型，现有因果追溯方法无法定位具体专家贡献。本文提出专家感知的因果追溯框架，通过对Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base模型的事实回忆任务进行噪声扰动和专家级干预，识别关键专家层（第44层）和专家更新对正确预测的贡献。实验表明专家级干预能有效恢复事实偏好，揭示了MoE模型中专家路由机制与事实记忆的关联，为高效MoE推理提供了结构理解依据。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对稀疏MoE语言模型，现有因果追溯方法无法定位具体专家贡献。本文提出专家感知的因果追溯框架，通过对Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base模型的事实回忆任务进行噪声扰动和专家级干预，识别关键专家层（第44层）和专家更新对正确预测的贡献。实验表明专家级干预能有效恢复事实偏好，揭示了MoE模型中专家路由机制与事实记忆的关联，为高效MoE推理提供了结构理解依据。

quick_skim.title: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Reward Models Learn Interpretable and Specialized Experts for Personalized Preference Modeling
quick_skim.abstract: Preference modeling plays a central role in reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), enabling large language models (LLMs) to align with human values. However, most existing approaches assume a universal reward function, neglecting the diversity and heterogeneity of human preferences. To address this limitation without additional annotation costs, recent work has proposed learning multiple preference components from binary data and combining them to model individual preferences. Nevertheless, these components often fail to capture coherent and disentangled patterns, limiting their interpretability and effectiveness for personalization. In this work, we propose a sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) reward model that encourages sparse routing and expert diversity during training on binary preference data. Across controlled and real-world experiments, sparse MoE learns interpretable routing patterns and specialized experts. It also improves test-time personalization, and post-adaptation shifts in expert weights provide a qualitative lens for analyzing how the model adapts to personalized preferences.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts reward models for personalized preference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 稀疏混合专家奖励模型用于个性化偏好建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 稀疏混合专家奖励模型用于个性化偏好建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 稀疏混合专家奖励模型用于个性化偏好建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes sparse MoE reward models that learn interpretable and specialized experts for personalized preference modeling in RLHF, addressing the limitation of universal reward functions. Demonstrates improved alignment and interpretability.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有RLHF采用统一奖励函数，忽略用户偏好多样性。本文提出稀疏MoE奖励模型，学习多个可解释的专家组件，每个组件捕捉一种偏好模式，然后动态组合以个性化建模。该方法无需额外标注，仅从二元偏好数据中训练。实验表明，该模型能产生更准确且可解释的偏好预测，为MoE结构在高效推理中的应用提供了新的激励。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有RLHF采用统一奖励函数，忽略用户偏好多样性。本文提出稀疏MoE奖励模型，学习多个可解释的专家组件，每个组件捕捉一种偏好模式，然后动态组合以个性化建模。该方法无需额外标注，仅从二元偏好数据中训练。实验表明，该模型能产生更准确且可解释的偏好预测，为MoE结构在高效推理中的应用提供了新的激励。
